req.title: Data retention (audit)
req.statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-095 mandates three verifiable behaviours: (1) expired resources are purged by the nightly retention job, (2) every purge operation emits an AuditEvent, and (3) a policy mismatch raises an incident. The positive case (EP) confirms the happy-path end-to-end flow. The boundary case (BVA) targets the retention-policy edge: a resource whose age equals exactly the retention threshold. The negative case (NEG) exercises the error pathway when the purge job encounters a policy mismatch and must raise an incident rather than silently deleting data. A fourth test using State Transition Coverage (STC) traces the full lifecycle state machine from 'active' through 'expired' to 'purged', and a fifth Decision Table (DT) test checks all combinations of expiry flag and audit-write success to guarantee correct incident behaviour.

tests.name: test_retention_job_purges_expired_resource_and_emits_audit_event
preconditions: fhir-lite service is running and reachable
An expired resource exists in the data store (retention date in the past)
Cron job endpoint /cron/retention is accessible with a valid service token
actions: Seed one resource with an expiry date 30 days in the past via <seed_expired_resource>
Poll /AuditEvent?entity=<resource_id> until event appears or timeout
expected: At least one AuditEvent record exists referencing the purged resource
trace: R-HLT-095: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"
HEADERS = {"Authorization": "Bearer <service_token>"}


def test_retention_job_purges_expired_resource_and_emits_audit_event(
    seed_expired_resource,
):
    resource_id = seed_expired_resource(days_past=30)

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=HEADERS)
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/Patient/{resource_id}", headers=HEADERS
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code in (404, 410), (
        f"Expected 404/410, got {get_resp.status_code}"
    )

    audit_url = f"{BASE}/AuditEvent?entity=Patient/{resource_id}"
    for _ in range(10):
        audit_resp = requests.get(audit_url, headers=HEADERS)
        bundle = audit_resp.json()
        entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
        if entries:
            break
        time.sleep(1)

    assert entries, "No AuditEvent found for purged resource"
    actions = [e["resource"].get("action") for e in entries]
    assert "D" in actions, f"Expected delete action in AuditEvents, got {actions}"

tests.name: test_retention_job_does_not_purge_resource_at_exact_boundary
preconditions: Retention policy is configured to N days (e.g. 90)
A resource exists whose expiry timestamp equals exactly now (boundary)
actions: Seed one resource with expiry timestamp set to current UTC second via <seed_boundary_resource>
GET the resource to verify presence
expected: GET /<ResourceType>/<resource_id> returns HTTP 200 (resource still present) OR 410 (just expired) — implementation-defined boundary semantics captured
If resource is purged, a corresponding AuditEvent with action 'D' must exist
If resource is retained, no delete AuditEvent must exist for that resource
trace: R-HLT-095: retention policy boundary — exactly-expired resource
R-HLT-095: audit trail consistency at boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"
HEADERS = {"Authorization": "Bearer <service_token>"}


def test_retention_job_does_not_purge_resource_at_exact_boundary(
    seed_boundary_resource,
):
    resource_id = seed_boundary_resource(offset_seconds=0)

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=HEADERS)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    time.sleep(2)

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/Patient/{resource_id}", headers=HEADERS
    )
    status = get_resp.status_code
    assert status in (200, 410, 404), f"Unexpected status {status}"

    audit_url = f"{BASE}/AuditEvent?entity=Patient/{resource_id}&action=D"
    audit_resp = requests.get(audit_url, headers=HEADERS)
    delete_events = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])

    if status in (404, 410):
        assert delete_events, (
            "Resource purged at boundary but no delete AuditEvent found"
        )
    else:
        assert not delete_events, (
            "Resource retained but spurious delete AuditEvent exists"
        )

tests.name: test_retention_job_raises_incident_on_policy_mismatch
preconditions: A resource exists whose retention policy tag is unknown/invalid, causing a mismatch
Incident tracking endpoint or log sink is observable
actions: Seed one resource with an invalid/unknown retention-policy label via <seed_policy_mismatch_resource>
Inspect response body or incident log for mismatch signal
expected: POST /cron/retention returns HTTP 207 Multi-Status or 200 with error detail, NOT 500
Response body or incident log contains a record referencing the mismatched resource ID
The mismatched resource is NOT deleted from the data store
An AuditEvent with outcome code indicating failure is emitted
trace: R-HLT-095: error pathway — incident raised on policy mismatch
R-HLT-095: audit trail written even for failed operations
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"
HEADERS = {"Authorization": "Bearer <service_token>"}


def test_retention_job_raises_incident_on_policy_mismatch(
    seed_policy_mismatch_resource,
    assert_incident_raised,
):
    resource_id = seed_policy_mismatch_resource()

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=HEADERS)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207), (
        f"Expected 200/207, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    body = resp.json()
    issues = body.get("issues", []) or body.get("errors", [])
    assert any(resource_id in str(i) for i in issues), (
        f"Policy mismatch not reported in response for {resource_id}"
    )

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/Patient/{resource_id}", headers=HEADERS
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200, (
        "Mismatched resource was incorrectly deleted"
    )

    assert_incident_raised(resource_id)

    audit_url = (
        f"{BASE}/AuditEvent?entity=Patient/{resource_id}&outcome=4"
    )
    for _ in range(10):
        ae = requests.get(audit_url, headers=HEADERS).json()
        if ae.get("entry"):
            break
        time.sleep(1)
    assert ae.get("entry"), "No failure AuditEvent found for mismatch"

tests.name: test_retention_job_state_transition_active_to_purged
preconditions: Resource lifecycle states are: active -> expired -> purged
A resource starts in active state with a future expiry
actions: Seed resource in active state via <seed_active_resource>
Advance system clock or update expiry to past via <expire_resource>
Confirm resource is now in expired state via metadata
Verify resource moves to purged state and AuditEvent exists
expected: After expiry update: resource metadata reflects expired status
AuditEvent chain covers all transitions with correct action codes
trace: R-HLT-095: full lifecycle state machine — active -> expired -> purged
R-HLT-095: audit trail covers every state transition
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"
HEADERS = {"Authorization": "Bearer <service_token>"}


def test_retention_job_state_transition_active_to_purged(
    seed_active_resource,
    expire_resource,
):
    resource_id = seed_active_resource()

    active_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/Patient/{resource_id}", headers=HEADERS
    )
    assert active_resp.status_code == 200
    assert active_resp.json().get("status") == "active"

    expire_resource(resource_id)
    meta_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/Patient/{resource_id}", headers=HEADERS
    )
    assert meta_resp.json().get("status") == "expired"

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=HEADERS)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    time.sleep(2)

    purged_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/Patient/{resource_id}", headers=HEADERS
    )
    assert purged_resp.status_code in (404, 410)

    ae_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/AuditEvent?entity=Patient/{resource_id}",
        headers=HEADERS,
    )
    events = ae_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(events) >= 1, "Expected at least one AuditEvent for purge"
    actions = {e["resource"].get("action") for e in events}
    assert "D" in actions

tests.name: test_retention_job_decision_table_expiry_and_audit_combinations
preconditions: Test can inject audit-write failure for a specific resource via <stub_audit_failure>
Two resources seeded: one normally expired, one expired with audit-write stubbed to fail
actions: Check purge and audit outcomes for both resources
expected: resource_A: purged (404/410) AND AuditEvent with action D present
resource_B: NOT purged (rollback on audit failure) AND an incident or error AuditEvent present
POST /cron/retention returns 200 or 207 — never a silent 500
trace: R-HLT-095: decision table — expiry x audit-write success/failure
R-HLT-095: incident raised on policy/audit mismatch
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

BASE = "http://localhost:8080"
HEADERS = {"Authorization": "Bearer <service_token>"}


def test_retention_job_decision_table_expiry_and_audit_combinations(
    seed_expired_resource,
    stub_audit_failure,
    assert_incident_raised,
):
    id_a = seed_expired_resource(days_past=10)
    id_b = seed_expired_resource(days_past=10)
    stub_audit_failure(id_b)

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE}/cron/retention", headers=HEADERS)
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    time.sleep(2)

    resp_a = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{id_a}", headers=HEADERS)
    assert resp_a.status_code in (404, 410), (
        f"resource_A should be purged, got {resp_a.status_code}"
    )
    ae_a = requests.get(
        f"{BASE}/AuditEvent?entity=Patient/{id_a}&action=D",
        headers=HEADERS,
    ).json().get("entry", [])
    assert ae_a, "resource_A purge must have a delete AuditEvent"

    resp_b = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{id_b}", headers=HEADERS)
    assert resp_b.status_code == 200, (
        f"resource_B must not be purged on audit failure, got {resp_b.status_code}"
    )
    assert_incident_raised(id_b)
